devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Device codes created by the OAuth device-authorization flow. Denying a code stops future polling for that code; it does not sign out an already-issued session.
devices.recentDescription: Pending entries are awaiting your approval. Approved entries can be marked denied here to prevent further use of the code.
devices.noCodesHint: When a CLI starts a device-authorization flow, the request shows up here.
devices.denyDescriptionNamed: Code {{code}} will be marked denied. The associated session, if any, is not signed out automatically.
devices.denyDescriptionUnnamed: The code will be marked denied.
devices.typeUserCodeToConfirm: Type the user code to confirm
devices.denyFailed: Couldn't deny that code. Try again.

overview.title: Admin
overview.description: Manage users, access, device sign-in, and instance settings.

observability.title: Global connection observability
observability.description: Read-only operational metadata for all CLI devices, endpoints, models, pools, and relays. Sensitive endpoint URLs, secrets, request bodies, responses, and stickiness digests are not shown.

seedPage.title: Run database seed
seedPage.description: Runs <0>runSeed()</0> from <0>packages/db/prisma/seed.ts</0> - the same function <0>prisma db seed</0> calls. This is a mechanism, not content: it does whatever your seed script does.
seedPage.emptyStubNote: The shipped seed script is an empty stub, so this does nothing until you write seed logic. Whatever you add must be idempotent - this button can be clicked repeatedly.
seedPage.prodWarningBody: This server is running in production. Seeding writes directly to the live database and cannot be undone. A stricter confirmation phrase is required.
seedPage.confirmDescription: This executes your seed script against the current database. Make sure it is idempotent before continuing.
seedPage.confirmDescriptionProd: PRODUCTION: this executes your seed script against the live database and cannot be undone. Only continue if you are certain the seed is idempotent and safe to run here.
seedPage.typePrompt: Type the phrase below to confirm
seedPage.jobEmpty: The seed returned no summary lines.

settings.title: Settings
settings.force2faDisabled: Two-factor authentication is no longer required.
settings.securityDescription: Controls that apply to every account on this instance.
settings.force2faDescription: Users must set up 2FA before using authenticated pages.
settings.force2faAdminWarning: Enable 2FA on your own account before requiring it for everyone.
settings.signupDescription: When disabled, new account creation is blocked after the first admin account exists.

media.title: Media policy
media.description: Ephemeral upload storage, retention, and cleanup for chat attachments.
media.actionFailed: That media action didn't work. Try again in a moment.
media.uploadStatusEnabled: Upload storage is configured on this server.
media.uploadStatusDisabled: Upload storage is not configured. Attachments fall back to base64 or external URLs, and purge actions are unavailable.
media.ttlDescription: How long a newly uploaded asset is kept before automatic cleanup removes its bytes and metadata.
media.ttlHint: Must be a whole number of hours between {{min}} and {{max}}.
media.ttlInvalid: Enter a whole number of hours.
media.ttlRange: Enter a value between {{min}} and {{max}} hours.
media.purgeDescription: Immediately delete assets whose retention window has passed, reclaiming disk without waiting for the hourly sweep.
media.deleteAllDescription: Permanently delete every stored asset's bytes and metadata for all users. This cannot be undone.
media.deleteAllConfirmDescription: This permanently removes all {{count}} stored asset(s) for every user. This cannot be undone.

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage accounts and roles for this instance.
users.deleteDescription: This permanently deletes the account. This cannot be undone.
users.typeEmailToConfirm: Type the user's email to confirm
users.inviteDescription: Create an account and reveal a temporary password. If SMTP is configured, the invite is emailed too.
users.revealEmailedDescription: The invite was sent to {{email}}. Copy the temporary password now in case delivery fails.
users.revealNotEmailedDescription: SMTP is not configured, so no email was sent to {{email}}. Share this temporary password manually.
users.tempPasswordHint: The user should change this password after signing in.
